---
title: "Understanding manifest.json"
description: "What every field in your extension's manifest file means."
---

# The Brain of Your Extension

The `manifest.json` file is the most important file in any Chrome extension. It's a configuration file that tells Google Chrome everything it needs to know to run your code properly. 

## Annotated Manifest (MV3)

Here is a look at a full Manifest V3 file with comments explaining each core key.

```json
{
  "manifest_version": 3,
  "name": "My Extensy Tool",
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "description": "A productivity extension generated by Extensy.",
  "action": {
    "default_popup": "popup.html",
    "default_icon": "icons/icon48.png"
  },
  "permissions": [
    "storage",
    "tabs"
  ],
  "host_permissions": [
    "https://*.google.com/*"
  ],
  "background": {
    "service_worker": "background.js",
    "type": "module"
  },
  "content_scripts": [
    {
      "matches": ["https://*.google.com/*"],
      "js": ["content.js"],
      "run_at": "document_idle"
    }
  ],
  "icons": {
    "16": "icons/icon16.png",
    "48": "icons/icon48.png",
    "128": "icons/icon128.png"
  }
}
```

## Core Key Breakdown

<AccordionGroup>
  <Accordion title="manifest_version">
    Must be set to `3`. This tells Chrome to use the Manifest V3 architecture.
  </Accordion>

  <Accordion title="name, version, description">
    These are the basic details shown in the browser's extension page and the Chrome Web Store.
  </Accordion>

  <Accordion title="action">
    Configures the toolbar icon and the popup window that appears when you click it.
  </Accordion>

  <Accordion title="permissions">
    An array of specialized **Chrome APIs** (like `storage`, `tabs`, `bookmarks`) that your extension needs to access.
  </Accordion>

  <Accordion title="host_permissions">
    Lists the specific websites your extension is allowed to run on or fetch data from.
  </Accordion>

  <Accordion title="background">
    Points to your "Service Worker" (`background.js`), which handles events even when your extension's UI is closed.
  </Accordion>

  <Accordion title="content_scripts">
    An array of scripts that are "injected" into the websites you visit to modify the DOM or read content.
  </Accordion>

  <Accordion title="icons">
    Points to the icon files used for the toolbar, the extensions management page, and the store listing.
  </Accordion>
</AccordionGroup>

<Info>
  **Important Note**: When Extensy generates your extension, we auto-fill these fields based on your prompt. You only need to touch this file if you're doing manual refinement!
</Info>

## Common Manifest Issues

1. **Incorrect Paths**: If you rename your `popup.html` or `background.js` but don't update the manifest, your extension will fail to load.
2. **Missing Permissions**: If your code calls `chrome.storage.set()` but `storage` isn't in your permissions array, you'll see a console error.
3. **Invalid JSON**: A missing comma or an extra quote will cause a "Manifest file is unreadable" error.

<Tip>
  Before uploading to the Chrome Web Store, we recommend using Google's [official manifest validator](https://developer.chrome.com/docs/extensions/mv3/manifest/) to ensure your configuration is perfect.
</Tip>

<Warning>
  **Permission Bloat**: Never add permissions your extension doesn't use. Each additional permission increases the chance that a user (or the Chrome Web Store reviewer) will feel uneasy about installing your tool.
</Warning>
